title: Provider capability admission — platform-admin disposition over a provider's DECLARED capabilities/categories as an intersecting ceiling (effective = declared ∩ admitted); immutable audit-chain record; enforced via the one Governance Matrix, gated by RBAC + a PROF-007-style approval workflow
rationale: ADR-PROV-002 established that a provider DECLARES capabilities (verb × domain) and occupies derived capability categories. But a declaration is only a CLAIM of ability — a provider must not, merely by declaring a capability, be permitted to USE it. Platform admins need to disposition each declared capability/category, per provider: pending → approved | provisional | denied, with optional SCOPE (admit a verb only over some domains/tenants/zones). Three parallel research sweeps (UDLM machinery, DCM RBAC/workflows, external prior art), citations verified, found the substrate already holds almost every part: the 'effective = declared ∩ permitted' intersection (effective_accepts_roles; data-roles.md 'delivered = accepts_roles ∩ Governance-Matrix-permitted … never widen beyond'; federation provisional 'cannot exceed voucher's scope'); the one Governance Matrix as enforcement, with capability + capability_category now first-class match sources on subject/target (ADR-PROV-002) and decisions ALLOW | DENY | ALLOW_WITH_CONDITIONS; the Accreditation lifecycle as an admin-disposition state machine (proposed → platform-admin approves → active → suspended/revoked/pending_review) WITH a scope block; the PROF-007 approval workflow (profile-scoped approver sets — standard 1, prod +security_owner, fsi/sovereign +compliance_officer + dual — P7D shadow, POLICY_PROVIDER_ELEVATED audit); RBAC via role-mapping with platform_admin already gating every provider admin verb; and a federation trust posture 'provisional' meaning 'cryptographically/structurally verified but NOT yet manually approved → a restricted operation subset (catalog_query only)'. External prior art converges on the SAME invariant: AWS permission-boundary/SCP (allowed only if identity-policy ∩ boundary ∩ SCP all allow), OAuth scope (granted MUST be a subset of requested; the server echoes the granted set back), OAuth RAR RFC 9396 (structured verb=actions × domain=locations/datatypes), OLM operator-RBAC clipped by a scoped service account (effective = declared ∩ SA-ceiling), and admission-policy Audit/Deny + OLM installPlanApproval:Manual as the provisional/staged mechanism. The ONE genuine gap is the BINDING: a per-(provider × capability/category) admission disposition — admission today is whole-provider (SUBMITTED → PENDING_APPROVAL → ACTIVE). The keystone principle, shared by substrate and prior art: an admin's approval is an intersecting CEILING, not a grant — a provider can never do more than it declared, nor more than an admin admitted; the effective set is COMPUTED as the intersection, never an independent grant list you could forget to reconcile. This buys least-authority, safe defaults (a newly declared capability is inert until admitted), and auditability ('why can provider P do X?' = show both sides of the intersection). Finally (Maintainer, 2026-07-11): admin changes MUST be audited and PERMANENT in the record — the disposition is immutable / append-only; every admin action (admit/provision/scope/deny/revoke/re-scope) is an explicit FORWARD audit event carrying actor + reason + timestamp + prior and resulting disposition, never a destructive overwrite; the current disposition is reconstructed LIFO from the audit chain. This is the exact durability model already adopted for relationships (SPEC-DESIGN-REQUIREMENTS §18a-e; audit-record.schema.json relationship_mutation; UDLM immutability + supersede) — reused, not reinvented.
question_refs: Maintainer 2026-07-11: give platform admins the ability to approve/provisional/scope/disapprove usage of a provider's declared capabilities and categories — a new matrix, perhaps best served by RBAC. Research it.
Maintainer 2026-07-11: ensure admin changes are audited and PERMANENT in the record.

options_considered.option: A new dedicated admission MATRIX (a second enforcement path)
options_considered.pros: a clean grid of (provider × capability) → disposition
options_considered.cons: the substrate mandates ONE Governance Matrix and 'no parallel enforcement paths'; a second matrix duplicates evaluation and drifts from the first
options_considered.rejected_because: violates the single-enforcement-surface invariant; the grid is a conceptual view, realized as records that SOURCE the one matrix

options_considered.option: A pure RBAC grant model (admin GRANTS capabilities to the provider)
options_considered.cons: RBAC is additive-allow — no deny, no provisional tier, and (critically) NO notion of a declaration to intersect against; a grant list can exceed what the provider declared. K8s base RBAC and OLM's grant-all default fail exactly here; the Service Catalog lesson: RBAC-visibility alone did not enforce admission
options_considered.rejected_because: loses the intersecting-ceiling invariant and the provisional/deny dispositions

options_considered.option: Whole-provider admission only (status quo: PENDING_APPROVAL → ACTIVE)
options_considered.pros: already exists; one decision per provider
options_considered.cons: too coarse — cannot admit storage-provisioning while denying compute-provisioning for the same provider; the InfoBlox multi-capability case (realize_resources/Network + serve_data/Network) has no per-capability disposition
options_considered.rejected_because: the requirement is explicitly per declared capability/category

options_considered.option: Assembled model — a per-(provider × capability/category) admission DISPOSITION record (intersecting ceiling), enforced via the existing Governance Matrix, gated by existing RBAC + a PROF-007-style approval, provisional reusing the federation posture, immutable audit-chain history (chosen)
options_considered.pros: reuses ~every mechanism already in the substrate; the only net-new is the binding record + a capability-admission audit event; consistent with effective_accepts_roles, the accreditation lifecycle, PROF-007, and the relationship durability model; matches IAM/OAuth prior art (adopt-by-reference)
options_considered.cons: introduces a per-capability disposition object and a new audit event family; requires deciding the record's home

decision: (a) DEFAULT-DENY: by default NO use of a provider is allowed. A declared capability/category grants NO authority and is UNUSABLE until explicitly admitted — `effective_capabilities` starts EMPTY; a `pending` capability is not usable. Declaring is a claim, not a permission. (a2) PROFILE-GOVERNED DEFAULT POSTURE ('default safe'): the default admission STRINGENCY — approver requirements, whether any capability may auto-admit, shadow-period length — is derived from the platform profile(s) in use (minimal → dev → standard → prod → fsi → sovereign), reusing the PROF-010/PROF-007 profile-scoped approver ladder and `default_approval_method`. Stricter profiles harden the gate; NO profile weakens the default-deny baseline (a capability is never usable while unadmitted). 'Default safe' is the profile's security default, not a fixed global. (b) DISPOSITION per (provider × capability/category): `pending | approved | provisional | denied`. `provisional` reuses the federation posture semantics — structurally/cryptographically verified but not manually approved → a restricted/shadowed subset, promotable to approved. TWO LEVELS, separated by concern: the ADMIN disposition is PLATFORM-LEVEL and COARSE (is this provider's capability/category admitted at all, platform-wide?); GRANULAR / conditional approval (per-tenant, per-zone, per-resource, contextual) is POLICY — Governance-Matrix rules (ALLOW_WITH_CONDITIONS), the `matrix-permitted` term of the intersection — NOT an admin-disposition field. There is therefore NO scope field on the admin disposition: domain granularity is already inherent (a category IS verb × domain, so an admin approves `realize_resources/Storage` independently of `realize_resources/Compute`), and tenant/zone/conditional narrowing is the policy layer. (c) INVARIANT (intersecting ceiling): `effective_capabilities = declared ∩ admitted ∩ registry-enabled ∩ Governance-Matrix-permitted` — COMPUTED, mirroring `effective_accepts_roles`; never an independent grant list; a provider can never exceed either its declaration or its admission. ADOPT BY REFERENCE the IAM permission-boundary / OAuth-scope intersection semantics (SPEC-DESIGN adopt-by-ref) and OAuth RAR (RFC 9396) as the structured verb × domain shape. (d) SYSTEM OF RECORD (DECIDED 2026-07-11, Maintainer): bind the disposition to the provider via the DCM-assigned registration verdict — it is already DCM-owned, already feeds the Governance-Matrix target axis, and keeps the intersection where effective_accepts_roles already lives. The verdict gains `capability_admissions[]` (per capability/category: platform-level disposition; granular scoping is policy, not stored here) and a computed `effective_capabilities`. (A standalone CapabilityAdmission artifact shaped like the accreditation lifecycle was the considered alternative — rejected to avoid a second artifact + join; the verdict's history is made permanent via the audit chain per (g), which gives the same durable per-capability history without a new type.) (e) ENFORCEMENT (two levels, one surface): an `approved` admission SOURCES an `ALLOW` rule keyed on `target.provider_uuid` + `capability_category` (`provisional`→`AUDIT_ONLY` shadow, `denied`→`DENY`); GRANULAR/conditional approval is authored as additional POLICY rules (`ALLOW_WITH_CONDITIONS` narrowing per tenant/zone/resource/context) — the policy layer, not the admin disposition. NOT a parallel matrix (single enforcement surface preserved). (f) WORKFLOW + RBAC: admin actions gate on the existing `platform_admin` role and reuse the PROF-007 approval pattern — profile-scoped approver sets (standard 1; prod +security_owner; fsi/sovereign +compliance_officer + dual approval), P7D shadow for the provisional→approved promotion. (g) AUDIT + PERMANENCE: the disposition is IMMUTABLE / append-only; every admin change (admit / provision / deny / revoke) is an explicit FORWARD audit event carrying actor, reason, timestamp, and prior + resulting disposition — never a destructive edit; the current disposition is reconstructed LIFO from the audit chain (SPEC-DESIGN §18a-e). Add an audit-record `capability_admission` action_detail shape (parallel to `relationship_mutation`) carried by a new `CAPABILITY_ADMIT` audit action — the capability-admission event family, sibling of `POLICY_PROVIDER_ELEVATED`. (h) EASY / STREAMLINED APPROVAL (empower the engineering team, don't block): default-deny is the FLOOR (nothing runs without an audited admission), but the admission STEP is made frictionless three ways — (i) PROFILE-DEFAULT AUTO-ADMIT: a low-stringency profile (minimal / dev / homelab) auto-admits a registered provider's declared capabilities, recording an automatic CAPABILITY_ADMIT (actor=system, disposition=approved, reason='<profile> default') — audited, not manual, so the floor holds while onboarding stays one step; prod / fsi / sovereign still require explicit admin admission; (ii) BULK ADMIT-ALL: one admin action admits every declared capability of a provider at once (`…/capabilities:admit-all`); (iii) TRUSTED-PROVIDER FAST-PATH: a provider whose attestation yields trust_posture=verified may be auto-admitted at registration where the profile permits. This REFINES (a2): a profile never weakens the audited-admission floor, but MAY automate the admission decision — which is exactly what keeps default-deny from blocking the UC-3/4/17 provider-realize path.

abstraction_decomposition.data: A per-(provider × capability/category) admission DISPOSITION {pending | approved | provisional | denied} at PLATFORM level — NO scope field on the disposition (granular narrowing is policy; domain granularity is inherent because a category IS verb × domain). Bound to the provider via the registration verdict. Default-deny: at registration each declared capability is recorded `pending` and `effective_capabilities` starts EMPTY. IMMUTABLE / append-only: the current disposition is a LIFO reconstruction of the audit chain, never a mutable field — reusing the relationship durability model (SPEC-DESIGN §18a-e; audit-record.schema.json). `effective_capabilities` is a COMPUTED intersection (declared ∩ admitted ∩ registry-enabled ∩ Governance-Matrix-permitted), mirroring effective_accepts_roles — not stored as an independent grant.
abstraction_decomposition.policy: TWO LEVELS. (1) The ADMIN disposition (platform level, coarse) is the `admitted` term — authored by a platform admin, gated by RBAC + a profile-governed approval workflow (PROF-010/PROF-007; stringency set by the platform profile — 'default safe'). (2) GRANULAR / conditional approval is POLICY — Governance-Matrix rules (the `matrix-permitted` term) narrowing per tenant / zone / resource-type / context via ALLOW_WITH_CONDITIONS. The one Governance Matrix is the enforcement/decision point (PDP, provider-contract §1a.8): an approved admission SOURCES an ALLOW rule on target.provider_uuid + capability_category; granular policy rules narrow it further. The intersecting-ceiling invariant holds across both: a rule may only NARROW (deny/condition), never widen, what was declared + admitted. provisional → AUDIT_ONLY shadow until promoted. Default-deny: absent an admission, nothing is permitted. Every admin action + every matrix evaluation is audited.
abstraction_decomposition.provider: The provider DECLARES its capabilities once (ADR-PROV-002) and holds NO authority from declaring — default-deny: every declared capability is unusable until admitted. The provider learns its effective (admitted) set back from DCM (the OAuth 'server echoes the granted scope' pattern) and can never invoke beyond it. A provider-initiated capability amendment re-enters pending; the provider cannot self-admit or self-provision (mirrors ADR-022 / trust: never self-declared).

produces: contracts/provider-contract.md (registration verdict gains capability_admissions[] — platform-level disposition, NO scope [granular = policy]; effective_capabilities as the computed intersection; a default-deny PRV-* requirement)

registry/audit-record.schema.json (new CAPABILITY_ADMIT action + $defs.capability_admission action_detail; append-only; actor + reason + prior + resulting disposition; enforced conditionally like relationship_mutation)

dcm: architecture/convergence-engine/policy-evaluation.md + Governance Matrix (admission → matrix-rule sourcing on target.provider_uuid + capability_category; ALLOW_WITH_CONDITIONS as the scope shape)
dcm: architecture/governance-enforcement/policy-profiles.md (generalize PROF-007 to capability admission — profile-scoped approvers + P7D shadow for provisional→approved; platform_admin RBAC gate)
contracts/capability-discovery.md (state inert-until-admitted + effective = declared ∩ admitted; adopt IAM/OAuth intersection + RAR by reference)

notes.author: claude-opus-4-8 (session a3cae804)
notes.text: Answers 'give admins approve/provisional/scope/disapprove over a provider's declared capabilities/categories — new matrix or RBAC?' Answer: NEITHER purely — a thin admin DISPOSITION layer, effective = declared ∩ admitted (intersecting ceiling), enforced via the ONE Governance Matrix, gated by existing platform_admin RBAC + a PROF-007-style approval, provisional reusing the federation posture, history immutable via the audit chain (Maintainer: audited + permanent). Assembled from parts that mostly EXIST (3 research sweeps, citations verified): effective_accepts_roles intersection, Governance Matrix capability/category match sources (ADR-PROV-002), accreditation-lifecycle disposition state machine + scope block, PROF-007 workflow, federation 'provisional' posture. Genuine gap = the per-(provider × capability) binding + a capability_admission audit event. Prior art adopted-by-reference: AWS permission-boundary/SCP + OAuth scope (effective = requested ∩ granted; echo granted back), OAuth RAR RFC 9396 (structured verb × domain), OLM scoped-SA + installPlanApproval:Manual, admission-policy Audit/Deny (= provisional). REFINED (Maintainer 2026-07-11): TWO LEVELS — admin approves a capability/category at PLATFORM level (coarse: approved/provisional/denied, the `admitted` term); POLICIES address GRANULAR approval (per tenant/zone/resource/context, the `matrix-permitted` term, ALLOW_WITH_CONDITIONS). NO scope field on the admin disposition (domain granularity is inherent — category IS verb×domain; tenant/zone narrowing is policy). DEFAULT-DENY: by default no provider use is allowed, effective_capabilities starts empty. 'DEFAULT SAFE' = profile-governed: admission stringency derives from the platform profile(s) in use (minimal→sovereign), never weakening default-deny. RESOLVED (Maintainer 2026-07-11): record home = registration-verdict extension (capability_admissions[] + computed effective_capabilities); standalone artifact rejected. Audit-permanence (Maintainer): immutable/append-only, admin changes = explicit forward audit events, LIFO reconstruction — reuses the relationship durability model. HELD; realization sweep not yet written.
